description_long: This is a hand axe with a straight, elongated wooden handle and a single, broad metal blade. The handle is a light brown color, appears to be made of wood, and ends in a pointed tip opposite the blade. The blade is metallic, wedge-shaped, and has a slightly curved cutting edge, with a matte, silvery finish. The blade is mounted perpendicularly to the handle and is secured with a visible collar or socket at the top of the handle. There are no visible engravings, markings, or decorative elements on either the handle or the blade. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks in urban or outdoor environments.
description: A straight-handled axe with a broad, silvery metal blade and a pointed wooden handle, featuring a simple, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the axe, with the wide metal blade and the pointed wooden handle clearly visible.
description_view_1: Edge-on view showing the narrow profile of the blade and the handle, with the pointed tip of the handle most prominent.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other face of the blade and handle.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the back, showing the handle's pointed end and the thinness of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, wooden handle with a pointed end, round cross-section, no visible grip features, smooth finish.
attribute_blade_design: Single, broad wedge-shaped blade with a slightly curved edge, no beard or poll, matte metallic finish.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade socketed onto the top of the handle.
